items.title: Houthi attacks on Israel open new front in Mideast war, threaten Red Sea shipping
items.summary: Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels have entered the escalating war in the Middle East, claiming two missile launches at Israel. Their involvement in the monthlong conflict could further hurt global shipping should they again target vessels in the Bab el-Mandeb Strait off the Red Sea, a narrow waterway that has become all the more vital since Iran effectively shut the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: North Korea tests high-thrust engine for missile capable of striking US mainland
items.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong-un oversaw a test of a high-thrust, solid-fuel engine for weapons and hailed it as a development to boost the country’s strategic military capability, state media reported Sunday. Analysts said the test signalled efforts to place multiple warheads on a single missile capable of reaching the United States mainland.

items.title: Middle East war live: Loud blasts rock Tehran, Iran threatens US universities in the Gulf
items.summary: Two powerful explosions shook northern Tehran early on Sunday as Iran threatened to carry out retaliatory strikes on US universities in the region and its Houthi allies in Yemen claimed a second missile attack on Israel. The Israeli army announced the death in combat of a fifth soldier in south Lebanon. Follow our live blog for the latest developments.

items.title: Double fears over Red Sea, Hormuz
items.summary: Worry continues to grow after Yemen’s Houthi rebels escalated their involvement in the conflict, with two missile fired toward Israel. The developments raise fresh fears that the group could again threaten Red Sea shipping routes, a vital global trade corridor, further increasing tensions across an already volatile regional conflict. Jean-Emile Jammine speaks with Kethevane Gorjestani, France 24's International Affairs editor, and Yousef Alshammari, President, London College of Energy Economics.

items.title: Yemen’s Houthis launch ballistic missile at Israel as Middle East conflict escalates
items.summary: Yemen’s Houthi rebels fired a ballistic missile towards Israel on Saturday, marking their entry into the intensifying Middle East war. The move comes amid rising regional tensions, US-Israeli airstrikes on Iran, and disruptions to Red Sea shipping that threaten global trade and energy markets.

items.title: Football fever comes to New York with unveiling of France's World Cup jersey
items.summary: The French Consulate in New York unveiled France's 2026 World Cup kits on Friday, featuring the mint-green away jersey, dubbed "Liberté", in tribute to the Statue of Liberty. The design symbolises the friendship between France and the US.

items.title: Senegal celebrate AFCON win at Stade de France after being stripped of title
items.summary: Senegal on Saturday paraded the Africa Cup of Nations trophy they have been ordered to return ahead of a friendly against Peru at the Stade de France in Paris. The Confederation of African Football (CAF) on March 17 reversed Senegal's 1-0 final victory over Morocco in Rabat on January 18 and awarded a 3-0 victory to the hosts.

items.title: Iranian strike on Saudi base wounds several US troops, damages aircraft
items.summary: A dozen US troops were wounded in an Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, with two suffering serious injuries and 10 concussions, according to US media reports. The personnel were inside a building hit during the strike, which involved at least one missile and several drones. The attack also caused damage to multiple US refuelling aircraft, according to US and Arab officials.

items.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill at least 5
items.summary: Russia launched over 270 drones at Ukraine overnight, killing at least five people, Ukrainian authorities reported on Saturday. Two people were killed and at least 11 more were injured in a nighttime Russian drone strike on Odesa, according to the head of the region, Serhii Lysak. According to Lysak's Telegram posts, the attack damaged a maternity hospital and private houses in the major Black Sea port city.

items.title: What could Houthi involvement in the Iran war mean?
items.summary: Iranian-backed Houthi rebels entered the month-old war in the Middle East on Saturday by claiming a missile launch that Israel said it intercepted. The Houthis’ entry could further hurt global shipping if they again target vessels in the Bab el-Mandeb Strait off the Red Sea. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani brings you this analysis.

items.title: Narcotrafic à Marseille : six personnes mises en examen après le meurtre de Mehdi Kessaci
items.summary: Six personnes ont été mises en examen et écrouées dans l'enquête sur l'assassinat à Marseille de Mehdi Kessaci, dont le frère Amine, figure de la lutte contre le narcotrafic, a été élu adjoint au maire samedi. La mort de ce jeune homme de 20 ans totalement étranger au trafic avait provoqué une onde de choc.

translation.title: Narcotrafic in Marseille: six people charged after Mehdi Kessaci's murder
translation.summary: Six people were charged and jailed in the investigation into the murder in Marseilles of Mehdi Kessaci, including Brother Amine, a figure in the fight against drug trafficking, was elected deputy mayor on Saturday. The death of this 20-year-old man who was totally foreign to trafficking had caused a wave of shock.

items.title: Côte d'Ivoire : arrêtées lors de la présidentielle, des centaines de personnes restent détenues
items.summary: Alassane Ouattara a été réélu en octobre avec près de 90 % des voix dès le premier tour. Selon les autorités, 1 658 personnes ont été arrêtées, essentiellement lors de manifestations interdites de l'opposition, et 394 d’entre elles ont déjà été condamnées pour trouble à l’ordre public. Amnesty International demande des "procès inéquitables". Des accusations que le ministère de la Justice dément.

translation.title: Ivory Coast: Arrested during the presidential elections, hundreds of people remain detained
translation.summary: Alassane Ouattara was re-elected in October with nearly 90 per cent of the votes in the first round. According to the authorities, 1,658 people have been arrested, mainly during banned demonstrations by the opposition, and 394 of them have already been convicted of disturbing public order. Amnesty International calls for "inequitable trials". Charges that the Department of Justice denies.

items.title: Mère et bipolaire : une BD pour briser le tabou sur la maladie
items.summary: À l’occasion de la Journée mondiale des troubles bipolaires, l’autrice Lila Albanese raconte dans une BD comment sa grossesse a été bouleversée par une dépression prénatale, avant qu’un diagnostic de bipolarité ne soit enfin posé. Elle dénonce en filigrane les injonctions faites aux femmes enceintes et lève le voile sur les tabous rencontrés par les futures mères bipolaires.

translation.title: Mother and bipolar: a comic book to break taboo on the disease
translation.summary: On the occasion of World Day of Bipolar Disorders, author Lila Albanese tells in a comic book how her pregnancy was disrupted by prenatal depression, before a diagnosis of bipolarity was finally made. She watermarks the injunctions given to pregnant women and raises the veil on taboos encountered by future bipolar mothers.

items.title: La Corée du Nord teste un moteur de missile à haut pouvoir propulsif
items.summary: Le dirigeant nord-coréen Kim Jong Un a assisté à un nouvel essai d'un moteur de missile à combustible solide dans le cadre du développement de l'arsenal stratégique du pays, a annoncé dimanche l'agence officielle nord-coréenne KCNA.

translation.title: North Korea tests high-power missile engine
translation.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong One attended a new test of a solid fuel missile engine as part of the development of the country's strategic arsenal, announced on Sunday the official North Korean agency KCNA.

items.title: Guerre au Moyen-Orient en direct : l'Iran menace les universités américaines dans les pays du Golfe
items.summary: Les Gardiens de la Révolution iraniens ont menacé dans la nuit de samedi à dimanche de frapper les universités américaines présentes dans les pays du Golfe, tandis que de fortes explosions étaient entendues à Téhéran. L'armée israélienne a annoncé la mort d'un cinquième soldat au Sud-Liban. Suivez la situation en direct sur notre live blog.

translation.title: War in the Middle East live: Iran threatens US universities in Gulf countries
translation.summary: The Iranian Revolutionary Guards threatened during the night of Saturday to Sunday to strike American universities in the Gulf countries, while heavy explosions were heard in Tehran. The Israeli army announced the death of a fifth soldier in southern Lebanon. Follow the live situation on our live blog.

items.title: Bénin, début de la campagne de la présidentielle du 12 avril prochain
items.summary: Au Bénin, la campagne présidentielle est bien lancée depuis ce vendredi. Après 10 ans au pouvoir, le président Patrice Talon boucle ses 2 mandats. À deux semaines du scrutin du 12 avril, les candidats entrent pleinement dans l’arène avec des stratégies et des moyens très différents. D’un côté, le candidat du camp présidentiel, Romuald Wadagni. De l’autre, Paul Hounkpè, son challenger. Il se déclare de l’opposition modérée.

translation.title: Benin, start of the presidential campaign on 12 April
translation.summary: In Benin, the presidential campaign has been well launched since Friday. After 10 years in power, President Patrice Talon completed his two terms. Two weeks before the April 12th election, candidates fully enter the arena with very different strategies and means. On one side, the presidential camp candidate, Romuald Wadagni. On the other hand, Paul Hounkpè, his challenger. He declared himself of moderate opposition.

items.title: Les Houthis du Yémen ciblent Israël, les frappes contre l'Iran continuent
items.summary: Pour la première fois depuis le début de la ⁠guerre au Moyen-Orient, les Houthis ⁠du Yémen, alliés de Téhéran, ont lancé samedi des missiles sur Israël. Après quatre semaines de conflit, le risque ​d'escalade à l'échelle de toute la région s'accroît un peu plus.

translation.title: Yemen's Houthis target Israel, Iran's strikes continue
translation.summary: For the first time since the beginning of the Middle East war, Yemen's Houthis, Tehran's allies, launched missiles on Israel on Saturday. After four weeks of conflict, the risk of escalation across the region increases slightly.

items.title: Drogue en Afrique de l’Ouest : pourquoi la région est devenue un hub mondial
items.summary:
L’Afrique de l’Ouest est aujourd’hui au cœur des routes mondiales du trafic de drogue. Entre explosion des saisies, démocratisation du crack et montée des drogues de synthèse, la région est devenue à la fois zone de transit et marché de consommation. Quelles sont les causes de cette mutation ? Quelles conséquences pour les États et les populations ?
 
Analyse avec Mouhamadou Kane, expert de la Global Initiative against Transnational Organized Crime.

translation.title: Drugs in West Africa: Why the region has become a global hub
translation.summary:
West Africa is now at the heart of the world's drug trafficking routes. Between the explosion of seizures, the democratization of crack and the rise of synthetic drugs, the region has become both a transit area and a consumer market. What are the causes of this mutation? What consequences for States and populations?

Analysis with Mouhamadou Kane, expert at the Global Initiative against Transnational Organized Crime.

items.title: Pierre Gervais: "depuis quelques mois, une partie de la base MAGA se sent trahie par Donald Trump"
items.summary: Des millions de personnes sont attendues dans les rues américaines pour une nouvelle journée "No Kings day". Journée de protestation contre la politique et les méthodes de Donald Trump que beaucoup comparent à un monarque absolu. Outre le camp démocrate et progressiste, le mécontentement populaire gagne aussi une petite frange de la base "MAGA", déçue pour diverses raisons par le président américain. Pierre Gervais, professeur de civilisation américaine, était l'Invité d'Au Cœur de l'Info.

translation.title: Pierre Gervais: "for a few months now, part of the MAGA base has felt betrayed by Donald Trump"

Day of protest against Donald Trump's politics and methods that many compare to an absolute monarch. In addition to the Democratic and Progressive camp, popular discontent also gains a small fringe of the "MAGA" base, disappointed for various reasons by the American president. Pierre Gervais, professor of American civilization, was the Invite of Au Coeur de l'Info.

translation.title: Learn the handbooks.
translation.summary: 5 June is World Environment Day and the environmental day established by our environmental protection laws. Twenty-two years ago, with the deployment of Xi Jinping, then Secretary-General of Zhejiang Province, the “millions of projects” focusing on the environmental rehabilitation of rural settlements were launched. At that time, Comrade Xi had been the secretary of the Zhejiang province for more than six months and had been working for a long time. Why does he focus on the rehabilitation of the rural habitat environment? At the grass-roots level, Comrade Xi found that while the Zhejiang economy was growing rapidly, the rural areas were faced with “growth problems” and the villages were less than satisfactory. Out of 34,000 villages in the province, only 4,000 have a better habitat. It was described as “a village after a village, which is full of garbage”. And the farmers described it as: “Getting up early and making money, counting it in the trash, spending it in the hospital.” It can be said that the remediation of the habitat environment was an urgent aspiration of the rural population of Zhejiang at the time. The fact that millions of projects focus on small problems such as what villages to follow and how to deal with animal and animal dung is a matter of life for the farmers. translation.title: Han is meeting with the United States delegation to the High-level Second Track Dialogue between China and the United States
translation.summary: On 5 June, the Vice-President of Xinhua, Han, met in Beijing with the United States delegation to the High-level Second Track Dialogue between China and the United States. Han is saying that Chinese-American relations are one of the most important bilateral relations in the world today. Currently, US-China relations are at a critical historical node. items.title: 全国人大常委会启动节约能源法执法检查
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 为推动节约能源法全面有效实施，促进更高水平、更高质量做好节能工作，全国人大常委会5日启动节约能源法执法检查。 节约能源法自1998年1月1日实施，并于2007年、2016年、2018年进行了修改。 记者5日从全国人大常委会节约能源法执法检查组第一次全体会议上了解到，为全面深入了解法律实施情况，此次执法检查采取实地检查与委托检查相结合的方式，检查组将赴天津、江苏、湖南、广东、广西、四川等省（区、市）开展实地检查，并委托北京、河北、山西、吉林、上海、重庆等省（市）人大常委会对本行政区域内节约能源法实施情况进行检查。检查组将采取实地检查、听取汇报、组织座谈、典型调研、专题研究、抽查暗访等多种方式，全面准确了解法律实施情况，对带有普遍性的问题深入调查研究。 检查组将重点检查8方面内容：落实节能管理制度情况，推进重点领域节能工作情况，重点用能单位节能管理情况，推动节能技术进步情况，实施节能激励措施情况，与法律配套的行政法规、部门规章以及地方性法规的制定情况，法律实施中存在的其他问题，对节约能源法修改的意见和建议。

translation.title: The Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress initiated an energy-saving law enforcement inspection
translation.summary: In order to promote the full and effective implementation of the Energy Conservation Act and to promote higher and higher-quality energy efficiency, the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress launched an energy conservation law enforcement inspection on 5 June. The Energy Conservation Act has been in force since 1 January 1998 and was amended in 2007, 2016 and 2018. On 5 May, journalists learned from the first plenary meeting of the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress ' s Standing Committee on Energy Conservation Law Enforcement Inspection Unit that, in order to obtain a comprehensive and in-depth understanding of the implementation of the law, the inspection took the form of a combination of on-site inspections and commissioned inspections, which will be carried out in the provinces (districts and municipalities) of Tianjin, Jiangsu, Hunan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Sichuan and Beijing, Hebei, Shanxi, Jilin, Shanghai and Chongqing, among others. The Unit will have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the implementation of the law and will conduct in-depth research and studies on issues of a general nature, in a variety of ways, including on-site inspections, debriefings, organization of talks, typical research, thematic studies, spot-checking and unannounced visits. The Unit will focus on eight areas: implementation of energy-saving management systems; promotion of energy-saving efforts in priority areas; focus on energy-saving management in energy-use units; promotion of technological progress in energy-saving; implementation of energy-saving incentives; development of administrative, sectoral and local regulations in conjunction with the law; other issues in the implementation of the law; and suggestions and recommendations for changes to the energy-saving law.

items.title: The Kremlin appears to be blocking Telegram. Meduza asked its Russia-based readers how well the crackdown is working.

He argued top programmers will become more valuable, not less, and that AI's biggest impact will be automating costly corporate operations like billing, accounting, and logistics.
